tldr: Ensemble of two fine-tuned ByT5 models via weighted weight averaging for Akkadian-to-English translation, using regex gap normalization and beam search inference.
summary: This notebook demonstrates an inference pipeline for machine translation from Akkadian to English using two fine-tuned ByT5 models. Instead of logit averaging, the author performs a weighted average of the model weights before loading them into a single model. The preprocessing pipeline replaces various ellipsis and gap patterns in the transliteration text with standardized tokens using regex. Inference is conducted with beam search and a max length of 512. The notebook serves as a practical recipe for model ensembling and text normalization in low-resource translation tasks.
extensive_summary:
## Setup and Imports
The notebook imports `pandas`, `torch`, `transformers`, `tqdm`, and `re`. It defines paths to two pre-trained/fine-tuned ByT5 models and sets inference parameters: `BATCH_SIZE=16`, `MAX_LENGTH=512`, and device selection.

## Preprocessing
A custom regex-based function `replace_gaps` normalizes various gap and ellipsis representations in the Akkadian transliteration data (e.g., `.3`, `xx`, `……`, `…`, `....`) into standardized `<big_gap>` or `<gap>` tokens. A reverse function `replace_gaps_back` is defined but not applied in the provided code. The translation prompt prefix `"translate Akkadian to English: "` is prepended to all inputs.

## Model Weight Averaging
Despite the markdown claiming logit averaging, the code performs weight averaging. Model weights are loaded, and a weighted sum is computed: `w1 = 0.94 / 1.94`, `w2 = 1.00 / 1.94`. The averaged weights are saved into `model2`'s state dict, effectively creating a single ensemble model.

## Inference Pipeline
A custom PyTorch `Dataset` tokenizes inputs with `max_length=512`, padding, and truncation. A `DataLoader` processes batches of size 16. Inference uses `model.generate` with `num_beams=4`, `early_stopping=True`, and `max_length=512`. Outputs are decoded with `skip_special_tokens=True` and stripped.

## Submission
Predictions are formatted into a DataFrame with `id` and `translation` columns. Empty predictions are replaced with `"broken text"`. The result is saved to `submission.csv`.
preprocessing: regex-based gap/ellipsis normalization to <big_gap> and <gap> tokens
tokenization with max_length=512, padding, and truncation
ensemble: Weighted averaging of model weights (w1=0.94/1.94, w2=1.00/1.94) before inference, despite the markdown claiming logit averaging.
notable_techniques: Regex normalization of Akkadian gap/ellipsis patterns to <big_gap> and <gap> tokens
